title: Make Light!
subtitle: Build a Simple LED Circuit Without Arduino
overview: In this session students build their first real electric circuit using only a battery, LED and wires. Students learn how electricity flows in a closed circuit and how LEDs glow when connected correctly.

images._note: Add photos from the actual classroom activity — battery, LED, wires, students testing circuits and glowing LEDs.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Photo of completed LED circuit
gallery.caption: Simple battery and LED circuit glowing successfully
LED converts electricity into light energy
Battery provides electrical energy to the circuit
Wires carry current from battery to LED
Open circuit — LED does not glow
Closed circuit — current flows and LED glows

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Photo of students performing activity
gallery.caption: Students creating their first electric circuit

keyTopics: What is a Circuit? — A complete path for electricity
Closed Circuit — Complete path allows current flow
Open Circuit — Broken path stops current flow
LED polarity — Long leg is positive (+)
Safety while working with batteries and wires

youtubeVideos.title: What is an Electric Circuit?
youtubeVideos.desc: Simple explanation of how electricity flows in a circuit.

activity.title: Battery + LED + Wire Circuit
steps: Identify the long and short legs of the LED
Connect the long leg of LED to battery positive terminal
Connect the short leg of LED to battery negative terminal using wire
Reverse LED connection and observe what happens

safetyRules: Do not connect wires directly for long time
Never use wall socket electricity for this activity
Do not touch exposed metal with wet hands

quiz.q: What is needed to make the LED glow?
quiz.explanation: LED glows only when electricity flows through a complete circuit.

quiz.q: What provides energy in the circuit?
quiz.explanation: Battery supplies electrical energy to the circuit.

quiz.q: What does an LED convert electricity into?
quiz.explanation: LED changes electrical energy into light energy.

quiz.q: What carries electricity in the circuit?
quiz.explanation: Wires allow current to flow between components.

quiz.q: Which circuit allows current to flow?
quiz.explanation: A closed circuit creates a complete loop for electricity.

quiz.q: What happens in an open circuit?
quiz.explanation: Broken connections stop the current flow.

quiz.q: Which LED leg is connected to positive terminal?
quiz.explanation: The longer leg of an LED is positive (+).

quiz.q: Why does the LED not glow when reversed?
quiz.explanation: LEDs allow current mainly in one direction.

quiz.q: Which is a real-life example of an LED circuit?
quiz.explanation: Torches use simple battery and light circuits.

quiz.q: What should students use for this activity?
quiz.explanation: Low-voltage batteries are safe for beginner activities.

quiz.q: What happens when the circuit loop is complete?
quiz.explanation: Current flows through the LED in a complete circuit.

quiz.q: What is the main purpose of wires?
quiz.explanation: Wires connect components and carry electricity.
Low-voltage batteries should be used carefully and safely.

quiz.q: What type of energy does the battery store?

quiz.explanation: Students understand the basics of electricity and circuits through hands-on learning.
